paragraphs.text: Public consultation Digital Fairness Act – VYV

According to the OFDT ESCAPAD survey carried out in 2022, 80.6 % of 17-year-olds have already experienced alcohol, 58.6 % have consumed alcohol in the month and 7.2 % have regular consumption (10 times in the month). Alarming figures are accompanied by an overall deterioration in mental health among adolescents.

evidence_quotes.quote: advertisers create a very strong sensory and emotional universe for this section of the population that they still know emotionally immature.

paragraphs.text: The PATH study carried out in 2013-2014 in the United States on the links between tobacco advertising and consumption shows that the appreciation of tobacco advertising strongly influences tobacco consumption among young adults aged between 18 and 24. For this reason, the PATH study recommends that preventive policies be put in place to limit exposure to tobacco and alcohol advertising and to promote counter-marketing messages to populations considered to be more vulnerable. In particular, advertisers create a very strong sensory and emotional universe for this section of the population that they still know emotionally immature. The prefrontal cortex is only mature from the age of 25.

evidence_quotes.quote: including through influencers active on all social networks.

paragraphs.text: In 2025, the ways in which young people, including minors, are exposed to advertising changed profoundly. They no longer go through traditional channels but are now deployed online, including through influencers active on all social networks. The problem is equally acute for alcohol, the promotion of which is often associated with festive and attractive representations. It is therefore crucial to strictly regulate these practices.

evidence_quotes.quote: several French influencers were heard demonstrating even more the dangerous and worrying nature of their behaviour on these platforms and their influence on minors.

paragraphs.text: In France, a parliamentary committee of inquiry into the psychological effects of TikTok on minors has recently highlighted the presence of harmful and shocking content, as well as the profit logic that guides the platform. In the framework of this committee of inquiry, several French influencers were heard demonstrating even more the dangerous and worrying nature of their behaviour on these platforms and their influence on minors.

evidence_quotes.quote: a ban on non-messaging social networks under the age of 15
the introduction of a digital curfew for 15-18 year-olds

paragraphs.text: 43 recommendations stem from this report, including a ban on non-messaging social networks under the age of 15 and the introduction of a digital curfew for 15-18 year-olds. These options should be further developed and developed at European level to protect all European citizens.

evidence_quotes.quote: Furthermore, the adoption of a European directive regulating the practices of influencers seems essential at this stage.
Such a directive would make it possible to better specify the acceptable limits of promotion for an influencer and to define its sector of commercial influence.

It would also better protect European consumers from the dangers of tobacco and alcohol, especially among the most vulnerable, such as minors who are overestimated with influencers' practices on several platforms: TikTok, Instagram, Snapchat... without management.

paragraphs.text: Furthermore, the adoption of a European directive regulating the practices of influencers seems essential at this stage. It could be inspired by the French law of 9 June 2023 on the framework of commercial influence and the fight against abuses of influencers on social media. Such a directive would make it possible to better specify the acceptable limits of promotion for an influencer and to define its sector of commercial influence. It would also better protect European consumers from the dangers of tobacco and alcohol, especially among the most vulnerable, such as minors who are overestimated with influencers' practices on several platforms: TikTok, Instagram, Snapchat... without management.

In order to protect minors, it would also seem necessary to strengthen prevention and awareness in order to try to change existing representations, change perceptions, misconceptions, behaviours and reduce the consumption of harmful products pending a strengthening of the regulatory framework. The Member States, supported by the European Union, must develop genuine digital and health education for the young population, integrating this dimension from school onwards and linking it with the digital spaces where young people spend most of their time. An interesting example is the French project "Les Gardiens du numérique", run by the VYV Group in partnership with the CNIL and the Ministry of National Education. The programme raises the awareness of minors and their parents about the dangers of the internet and social media. This type of initiative should be encouraged and

evidence_quotes.quote: through responsible influencers identified as 'public health ambassadors'.

paragraphs.text: Finally, the creation of a massive information campaign for minors on the risks associated with the use of tobacco, alcohol, drugs and gambling would be particularly effective if it were based on the privileged communication channels of young people, in particular through responsible influencers identified as 'public health ambassadors'.

evidence_quotes.quote: the protection of children from exposure to alcohol or tobacco advertising

paragraphs.text: The VYV Group supports the proposals and initiatives of Addict'Aid in the fight against addiction and in particular the protection of children from exposure to alcohol or tobacco advertising.
The contribution of the Central European Lawyers Initiative (CELI) is provided in the attached document.
